full_prompt: Design a reusable capillary-blood lancing auto-injector body aimed at point-of-care glucose diagnostics. The device is a cylindrical handheld injector 120 mm long x 18 mm diameter, actuating a disposable 28G lancet (fingerstick) with a spring-driven linear motion. The body is injection-molded polycarbonate (yield 62 MPa, E 2.2 GPa, density 1200 kg/m^3) with a stainless steel internal spring rail and a 28G lancet holder cartridge. The injector must deliver a lancet tip stroke of 1.8 mm +/- 0.2 mm into the fingertip with a contact velocity of 2.0 m/s +/- 0.2 m/s, powered by a 14 N nominal compression spring with a free length of 20 mm. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) housing max von Mises stress during the post-fire end-stop impact of the piston at 2.0 m/s against the forward end-stop must not exceed 41 MPa (PC yield 62 / SF 1.5), (R2) actuation-button breakaway force to fire the injector must be 4-8 N per ISO 11608-1 sharps safety human-factors band, (R3) the lancet must be retracted fully inside the housing within 50 ms of firing and must physically not be re-fireable without loading a new cartridge (safety lockout), (R4) drop test on the device from 1.0 m onto a hard surface (equivalent quasi-static 200 N impulse through the housing) must not produce housing max von Mises above 41 MPa and must retain the lancet, and (R5) total device mass must not exceed 30 g per commercialization ergonomic target. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with housing, spring, piston, cartridge interface, and trigger as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing housing stress, actuation force, retraction time, drop result, and total mass.
short_prompt:
Design a reusable capillary-blood lancing auto-injector body for point-of-care glucose diagnostics. The device is a cylindrical handheld injector roughly 120 mm long x 18 mm diameter in injection-molded polycarbonate with a stainless internal spring rail, firing a disposable 28G lancet cartridge. Stroke target is 1.8 mm +/- 0.2 mm at a 2.0 m/s +/- 0.2 m/s contact velocity, driven by a 14 N nominal compression spring (20 mm free length).

Design to ISO 11608-1 (needle-based injection systems, sharps safety and ergonomics). Body is held by the user at the grip section; lancet contacts fingertip tissue at fire.

Load cases.
- LC1 end-stop impact: piston hits the forward end-stop at 2.0 m/s.
- LC2 actuation-button breakaway: required firing force band 4-8 N.
- LC3 retraction lockout: lancet must be fully retracted within 50 ms and not re-fireable without a new cartridge.
- LC4 1 m drop (equivalent 200 N impulse) onto hard surface, lancet retained.

Total device mass is an ergonomic constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Spring-to-piston velocity and contact-velocity envelope are closed-form energy-conservation, not FEA.

prompt.title: Reusable capillary lancing auto-injector
prompt.brief: Design a 120 mm x 18 mm reusable PC body lancing auto-injector with 1.8 mm +/- 0.2 mm stroke at 2.0 m/s contact velocity, 4-8 N actuation breakaway, safety lockout, 1 m drop survival, and 30 g ergonomic cap.

boundary_conditions.body: held by user at grip section

objective.primary: pass ISO 11608-1 sharps safety + stroke envelope

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: PC yield 62 / 1.5 under 1 m drop quasi-static equivalent

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Handheld, one-time firing; no harmonic loads.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: No fluid domain; lancet-tissue contact treated as contact BC.

eval_coverage: explicit dynamic end-stop impact with PC yield limit
drop test integrity as safety pass/fail
